summary: webpack-patch fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com via Function constructor
details: webpack-patch@1.1.8 impersonates the pino logger (keywords, filenames pino.js/transport.js, module.exports.pino API surface) while shipping a remote-code loader in lib/caller.js. When the exported middleware is invoked, index.js spawns lib/caller.js as a detached, stdio-ignored, unref'd Node child process. That child issues an axios GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/D3GLF (with fallbacks https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3 and https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK), reads the `cookie` field from the JSON response, and executes it via `new Function.constructor("require", s)` with the real `require` passed in — granting the response author arbitrary code execution with full Node module access on the consumer's machine. The remote URLs and headers are base64-encoded and stored under decoy names like DEV_API_KEY/DEV_SECRET_KEY in lib/const.js to disguise the C2 configuration. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host, so the executed payload can be swapped at any time. The package name and API impersonate the widely-used pino/webpack ecosystem to lure installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
